examples: It promiscuously plunders other languages and delights in neologisms.
This young girl has to share a room with her mother who lives promiscuously.
It's like biology: in an ecosystem where microbes are promiscuously swapping genes and traits, evolution speeds up.
The deal Annan struck, which Clinton promiscuously clasped to his bosom, would strip the U.N.
New government regulations and internal risk-mandates will mean that credit won't flow as promiscuously.
